coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Saturday long run in taper: 12–14 miles with 1,200–1,400 ft of vert, all Z2 (140–145 bpm). You've logged 27.1 miles this week already; this caps your taper volume responsibly without backing off the alpine stimulus. Your recent runs show solid HR control at 133–142 bpm on hill work—stay disciplined in Z2 today and don't drift into tempo. The Julian Alps 50K's sparse aid and relentless vert demand you stay comfortable on tired legs; this long, steady mountain effort builds that durability without accumulation cost.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Ingest 40–50g carbs per hour during this run (sports drink, gels, or rice balls—whatever you've tested at race effort). Your sleep efficiency is solid at 88.8%, but 5.62 hours is short; you're running a glycogen deficit. Front-load breakfast 2–3 hours pre-run with 80–100g carbs and 20g protein, then refuel aggressively post-run within 30 minutes (40g carbs + 15g protein) to support recovery in this final taper week. Hydration: 20–24 oz fluid per hour in the Slovenian heat you'll face in September.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your recovery score is 48/100 (moderate), which in taper usually signals accumulated fatigue—not alarm, but a clear signal to prioritize sleep and active recovery tomorrow. Tonight: aim for 7–8 hours (you're averaging 5.03h the past 30 days; that's not sustainable three weeks out). Ice bath or 10-minute cold plunge post-run if available; it'll blunt DOMS and support parasympathetic shift. Skip hard gym work; Sunday should be 4–6 easy miles or rest, nothing more.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 90 days out and in taper—this is where doubt creeps in. Your training load has dropped correctly (45.1 miles last week to 27.1 this week), which feels like detraining but is the opposite. Trust the plan. You've built the durability and vertical tolerance; these final three weeks sharpen your nervous system and top off glycogen. Today's run is high-quality, not high-volume. Show up, execute Z2 discipline, and let the work compound. Slovenia's waiting.
